{"description":"base_branch: main\nintegration_branch: integration/beads-ide\nThe Beads IDE is a greenfield local web application for designing, previewing, and analyzing workflow formulas and the beads they produce. It replaces editing .formula.toml files in a text editor with a purpose-built IDE featuring a formula editor, cook preview, and post-execution results analysis. The implementation follows a two-process architecture: a Vite + React SPA (frontend) and a lightweight Node.js API server (backend) that proxies bd/gt/bv CLI commands. There is no existing UI code in the BCC project — this is a net-new build. The openedi project at /home/krystian/gt/deacon/dogs/bravo/openedi/ provides the reference architecture for component patterns, styling, and tooling conventions."}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.1.2","title":"Define shared TypeScript types","description":"Create all shared TypeScript type definitions for the Beads IDE. Types are derived from docs/beads.md (Go Issue struct, lines 17-75) and docs/formulas.md (Go Formula struct, lines 1-250) and must match the actual JSON output shape of bd list --json and bd show --json.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/types.ts — core domain types: Bead, BeadStatus, BeadType, Dependency, DependencyType, Formula, VarDef, Step, Gate, LoopSpec, OnCompleteSpec, ComposeRules
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts — IDE-specific types: Wave, GraphNode, GraphMetrics, GraphStats, FormulaFile, CookResult, ApiResponse, CliInvocation, SessionState, BeadsIDEConfig
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/index.ts — barrel export for all types

Key details: Types are the single source of truth consumed by both frontend (all components) and backend (route handlers, CLI response parsing). Derive all type definitions directly from docs/beads.md lines 17-75 (Go Issue struct -> Bead type, status/type enums, dependency struct) and docs/formulas.md lines 1-250 (Go Formula struct -> Formula type, VarDef, Step, Gate, etc.). Run bd list --json | head -1 and bd show <any-id> --json to verify type shapes match actual CLI output.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All types compile with strict TypeScript
- [ ] Types match actual bd list --json output (verify with a manual JSON sample)
- [ ] Types exported and importable from both frontend and backend","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:05Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:05Z"}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.1.3","title":"Implement CLI wrapper with security","description":"Create a secure backend utility for invoking bd, gt, and bv CLI commands without shell injection vulnerabilities. This wrapper is used by all backend route handlers.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts — execFile wrapper with allowlist validation
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/cli.test.ts — unit tests

Key details:
- Use Node.js child_process.execFile (NOT exec) — no shell interpolation
- Validate inputs: formula names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$, variable keys against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$, variable values against ^[^\x00-\x1f]+$ (no control chars)
- Set cwd to project root where .beads/redirect resolves
- Default timeout: 30s; cook timeout: 60s
- Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
- CLI flag verification (prerequisite for 2A.1/2A.3): Run bd cook formulas/explore-module.formula.toml --json and check if stdout is valid JSON. Run bv --export-graph - --output-format json and check if stdout is JSON (not HTML). If --json flag is not recognized by bd cook, fallback: parse the plain-text stdout from bd cook line-by-line. If --output-format json is not supported by bv --export-graph, fallback: bv may write an HTML file — parse the graph data from the HTML or use bv --robot-insights --output-format json only. Document findings in beads-ide/docs/cli-flags.md.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Rejects formula names with shell metacharacters (; | & \` $)
- [ ] Uses execFile not exec (no shell)
- [ ] Tests cover: valid invocation, rejected input, timeout, non-zero exit
- [ ] CLI flag verification: confirm bd cook --json and bv --export-graph --output-format json produce expected output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:05Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:05Z"}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.1.5","title":"Set up Vite frontend skeleton","description":"Create a minimal React application with TanStack Router, Tailwind CSS v4, and the app shell structure. This skeleton is the foundation for all frontend feature work. Follow openedi's Vite config pattern at /home/krystian/gt/deacon/dogs/bravo/openedi/.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/vite.config.ts — dev server on port 5173, proxy /api -> localhost:3001, @/ path alias
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/main.tsx — React root + TanStack Router + Sonner Toaster
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/app.css — Tailwind v4 imports, brand-* CSS variables, dark mode
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx — root layout (placeholder shell)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/index.tsx — landing page (placeholder)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/utils.ts — cn() utility (clsx + twMerge)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts — typed fetch wrapper returning ApiResponse<T>
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/tsconfig.json — extends base config, includes path aliases

Key details: Named exports only (no default exports). Biome for formatting/linting. The api.ts fetch wrapper handles connection errors globally using the ApiResponse<T> envelope pattern.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] npm run dev starts frontend on localhost:5173
- [ ] Root route renders a placeholder page

attached_at: 2026-02-22T02:18:45Z
dispatched_by: bcc/crew/ed

Create the Hono HTTP server that serves as the backend for the Beads IDE. The server proxies CLI commands and exposes a typed REST API to the frontend. It must bind to 127.0.0.1 only (not 0.0.0.0) per spec security requirements.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/index.ts — Hono app, bind 127.0.0.1:3001, register routes
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/config.ts — load formula search paths, CLI binary locations, project root detection
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts — GET /api/health verifies bd CLI is available
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tsconfig.json — extends base TypeScript config

Key details:
- Use @hono/node-server for the Node.js runtime adapter
- Config resolves formula search paths in order: formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/; skip missing directories gracefully
- GET /api/health returns { ok: true, bd_version: "..." }
- GET /api/config returns { formula_paths: [...], project_root: "...", ... }"}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.1.3","title":"Implement CLI wrapper with security","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-iulv
attached_at: 2026-02-22T02:10:22Z
dispatched_by: bcc/crew/ed

Create a secure backend utility for invoking bd, gt, and bv CLI commands without shell injection vulnerabilities. This wrapper is used by all backend route handlers.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts — execFile wrapper with allowlist validation
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/cli.test.ts — unit tests

Key details:
- Use Node.js child_process.execFile (NOT exec) — no shell interpolation
- Validate inputs: formula names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$, variable keys against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$, variable values against ^[^\x00-\x1f]+$ (no control chars)
- Set cwd to project root where .beads/redirect resolves
- Default timeout: 30s; cook timeout: 60s
- Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
- CLI flag verification (prerequisite for 2A.1/2A.3): Run bd cook formulas/explore-module.formula.toml --json and check if stdout is valid JSON. Run bv --export-graph - --output-format json and check if stdout is JSON (not HTML). If --json flag is not recognized by bd cook, fallback: parse the plain-text stdout from bd cook line-by-line. If --output-format json is not supported by bv --export-graph, fallback: bv may write an HTML file — parse the graph data from the HTML or use bv --robot-insights --output-format json only. Document findings in beads-ide/docs/cli-flags.md.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Rejects formula names with shell metacharacters (; | & \` $)

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.1.3","title":"Implement CLI wrapper with security","description":"Create a secure backend utility for invoking bd, gt, and bv CLI commands without shell injection vulnerabilities. This wrapper is used by all backend route handlers.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts — execFile wrapper with allowlist validation
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/cli.test.ts — unit tests

Key details:
- Use Node.js child_process.execFile (NOT exec) — no shell interpolation
- Validate inputs: formula names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$, variable keys against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$, variable values against ^[^\x00-\x1f]+$ (no control chars)
- Set cwd to project root where .beads/redirect resolves
- Default timeout: 30s; cook timeout: 60s
- Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
- CLI flag verification (prerequisite for 2A.1/2A.3): Run bd cook formulas/explore-module.formula.toml --json and check if stdout is valid JSON. Run bv --export-graph - --output-format json and check if stdout is JSON (not HTML). If --json flag is not recognized by bd cook, fallback: parse the plain-text stdout from bd cook line-by-line. If --output-format json is not supported by bv --export-graph, fallback: bv may write an HTML file — parse the graph data from the HTML or use bv --robot-insights --output-format json only. Document findings in beads-ide/docs/cli-flags.md.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Rejects formula names with shell metacharacters (; | & \` $)
- [ ] Uses execFile not exec (no shell)
- [ ] Tests cover: valid invocation, rejected input, timeout, non-zero exit
- [ ] CLI flag verification: confirm bd cook --json and bv --export-graph --output-format json produce expected output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:30Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:30Z"}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.2.1","title":"Formula routes","description":"Implement the backend REST API routes for formula CRUD, cook, and sling operations. All CLI invocations use the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts with full input validation.

- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/formulas.test.ts — integration tests

- PUT /api/formulas/:name — write formula TOML to disk (target for auto-save)
- POST /api/formulas/:name/cook — invoke bd cook <path> --json, return CookResult

{"id":"bcc-6not9.2.3","title":"Graph routes","description":"Implement backend API routes that proxy bv CLI commands to expose graph metrics and graph export data. The backend normalizes bv output into typed GraphMetrics shapes defined in beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/graph.ts — graph route handlers
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/graph.test.ts — integration tests

Endpoints:
- GET /api/graph/metrics — invoke bv --robot-insights --output-format json
- GET /api/graph/export — invoke bv --export-graph - --output-format json (stdout export)

Key details: Parse bv output into GraphMetrics types. The 9 graph metrics are: PageRank, betweenness centrality, HITS (authority + hub), critical path length, eigenvector centrality, degree (in/out), density, cycle count, and topological sort order. Map each to a named property on the GraphMetrics interface from beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts. If bv binary is missing, return a structured error response (not a crash) — missing bv is a gracefully handled degraded state. Uses the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Returns 9 graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness, HITS, etc.)

{"id":"bcc-6not9.2.7","title":"Graph library evaluation","description":"Benchmark graph rendering performance with 200 nodes to select the graph library for Phase 4's graph visualization. The architecture decision table in the plan designates this as an explicit evaluation step before committing to a library.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/graph-benchmark.ts — test harness generating 200-node synthetic graphs (temporary spike code, not production)

Candidates to evaluate: React Flow, Cytoscape.js, D3.js force simulation.

Test parameters for each candidate:
- 200 nodes, ~300 edges (1.5 edges/node average)
- Force-directed layout
- Measure initial render time (target: <1s)
- Measure interaction latency — pan/zoom/drag (target: <100ms)
- Environment: Chrome, 1280x720

Also evaluate: hierarchical layout support, manual node positioning, click/hover event APIs.

Key details: The selected library is added to frontend dependencies and used in tasks 4B.1 (graph visualization) and 5.1 (visual formula builder). Write the decision record to beads-ide/docs/graph-library-decision.md with a table of library x metric results and a "Selected: [library name]" line at the top.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] At least one library meets <1s render target for 200 nodes
- [ ] Decision documented in beads-ide/docs/graph-library-decision.md with benchmark results table
- [ ] Library added to frontend dependencies","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:09Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:09Z"}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.3.1","title":"TOML text editor","description":"Build the CodeMirror 6 TOML editor that is the primary interface for editing .formula.toml files. This is the central component of the formula editor route and the foundation for the auto-save hook and variables panel.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/formulas/text-editor.tsx — CodeMirror 6 wrapper component
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/formula-parser.ts — TOML parse + validate against Formula type from beads-ide/packages/shared/src/types.ts
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/formula.\$name.tsx — formula editor route (TanStack Router file-based route)
- beads-ide/packages/shared/tests/formula-parser.test.ts — parser tests with real formula fixtures
- beads-ide/packages/shared/tests/fixtures/ — copies of BCC formula files for testing (e.g., explore-module.formula.toml)

Key details:
- CodeMirror 6 for the editor. For TOML syntax highlighting: search npm for codemirror-lang-toml or similar community packages. If no suitable TOML extension exists, fall back to plain text mode — TOML syntax highlighting is nice-to-have, not blocking. The smol-toml parser handles all structural validation regardless of editor highlighting.
- smol-toml library for TOML parsing (small, fast, ESM-native)
- Validate parsed TOML against Formula type shape; show inline diagnostics for parse errors
- Editor fills the main panel of the app shell
- Undo/redo via CodeMirror's built-in history extension
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Opens and renders real .formula.toml files (e.g., explore-module.formula.toml)

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.1.3","title":"Implement CLI wrapper with security","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-mu1ic
attached_at: 2026-02-22T02:35:03Z
dispatched_by: bcc/crew/ed

Create a secure backend utility for invoking bd, gt, and bv CLI commands without shell injection vulnerabilities. This wrapper is used by all backend route handlers.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts — execFile wrapper with allowlist validation
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/cli.test.ts — unit tests

Key details:
- Use Node.js child_process.execFile (NOT exec) — no shell interpolation
- Validate inputs: formula names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$, variable keys against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$, variable values against ^[^\x00-\x1f]+$ (no control chars)
- Set cwd to project root where .beads/redirect resolves
- Default timeout: 30s; cook timeout: 60s
- Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
- CLI flag verification (prerequisite for 2A.1/2A.3): Run bd cook formulas/explore-module.formula.toml --json and check if stdout is valid JSON. Run bv --export-graph - --output-format json and check if stdout is JSON (not HTML). If --json flag is not recognized by bd cook, fallback: parse the plain-text stdout from bd cook line-by-line. If --output-format json is not supported by bv --export-graph, fallback: bv may write an HTML file — parse the graph data from the HTML or use bv --robot-insights --output-format json only. Document findings in beads-ide/docs/cli-flags.md.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Rejects formula names with shell metacharacters (; | & \` $)
- [ ] Uses execFile not exec (no shell)
- [ ] Tests cover: valid invocation, rejected input, timeout, non-zero exit

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.1.3","title":"Implement CLI wrapper with security","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-mu1ic
attached_at: 2026-02-22T02:35:03Z
dispatched_by: bcc/crew/ed

Create a secure backend utility for invoking bd, gt, and bv CLI commands without shell injection vulnerabilities. This wrapper is used by all backend route handlers.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts — execFile wrapper with allowlist validation
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/cli.test.ts — unit tests

Key details:
- Use Node.js child_process.execFile (NOT exec) — no shell interpolation
- Validate inputs: formula names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$, variable keys against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$, variable values against ^[^\x00-\x1f]+$ (no control chars)
- Set cwd to project root where .beads/redirect resolves
- Default timeout: 30s; cook timeout: 60s
- Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
- CLI flag verification (prerequisite for 2A.1/2A.3): Run bd cook formulas/explore-module.formula.toml --json and check if stdout is valid JSON. Run bv --export-graph - --output-format json and check if stdout is JSON (not HTML). If --json flag is not recognized by bd cook, fallback: parse the plain-text stdout from bd cook line-by-line. If --output-format json is not supported by bv --export-graph, fallback: bv may write an HTML file — parse the graph data from the HTML or use bv --robot-insights --output-format json only. Document findings in beads-ide/docs/cli-flags.md.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Rejects formula names with shell metacharacters (; | & \` $)
- [ ] Uses execFile not exec (no shell)
- [ ] Tests cover: valid invocation, rejected input, timeout, non-zero exit
- [ ] CLI flag verification: confirm bd cook --json and bv --export-graph --output-format json produce expected output","notes":"Implemented: Fixed biome lint error in cli.ts (biome-ignore for intentional control char regex). Verified CLI flags: bd cook --json outputs valid JSON, bv --robot-graph --graph-format json outputs valid JSON. All 25 unit tests pass. CLI flag documentation already present in docs/cli-flags.md. All acceptance criteria met.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"bcc/polecats/chrome","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:30Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:56:09Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:56:09Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

- Filter dependencies to workflow types only: blocks, parent-child, conditional-blocks, waits-for

- Return { waves: Wave[], cycles: string[][], hasCycles: boolean }

Key details:
- Use @hono/node-server for the Node.js runtime adapter
- Config resolves formula search paths in order: formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/; skip missing directories gracefully

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.2.1","title":"Formula routes","description":"Implement the backend REST API routes for formula CRUD, cook, and sling operations. All CLI invocations use the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts with full input validation.

Files to create:

- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/formulas.test.ts — integration tests

Endpoints:
- GET /api/formulas — scan all configured search paths (formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/), return FormulaFile[] with name, path, directory
- GET /api/formulas/:name — read and return formula TOML content + parsed structure
- PUT /api/formulas/:name — write formula TOML to disk (target for auto-save)
- POST /api/formulas/:name/cook — invoke bd cook <path> --json, return CookResult
- POST /api/formulas/:name/sling — invoke gt sling <formula> <target>, return status

Key details: Formula names validated against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$ before any CLI invocation. Invalid names return 400 with error message.

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.5.2","title":"Error handling for all failure modes","description":"Implement robust error handling for all failure modes identified in the spec Error Handling table. Ensures the IDE degrades gracefully rather than showing blank screens or crashing.

Files to update:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts — connection error detection, offline/degraded mode signaling
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx — React error boundary

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/error-page.tsx — "Cannot connect to backend" full-page error
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/offline-banner.tsx — "Database unavailable" degraded mode banner

Failure mode matrix (from spec):
- Backend not running -> error page (not blank screen)
- Cook failure -> inline error with stderr in cook preview panel
- Sling failure -> toast.error() with retry
- Database unavailable -> degraded mode: formula editing still works, bead data unavailable banner shown
- Formula validation error -> inline highlighting in editor
- Wave cycle detected -> warning banner + list fallback

Key details: All fetch errors flow through beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts which returns ApiResponse<T> with { data, error } envelope. Use try/catch with finally for loading state reset (openedi pattern). User-facing errors via toast.error() (Sonner).

- [ ] Cook failure shows stderr inline
- [ ] Sling failure shows toast with error details
- [ ] Formula editing works even when database is down","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:40Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:40Z"}

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.5.3","title":"Accessibility pass (WCAG 2.1 AA)","description":"Conduct a comprehensive accessibility audit and remediation pass across all components to meet WCAG 2.1 AA. This is a cross-cutting pass over multiple component files in beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/.

- components/results/graph-view.tsx — list/tree alternative, aria-labels
- All icon-only buttons across components/ — add aria-label
- All loading states — add aria-live regions

Key details per spec:
- Screen reader alternative for graph: list/tree view accessible to screen readers
- Shape + icon as primary differentiators (not color alone) for node types and bead status
- Keyboard navigation for all operations (no mouse-only actions)
- Proper heading hierarchy throughout
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons
- aria-live regions for loading states

- Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- Terminology enforcement: verify "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", "dependency" not "bond"

Build the CodeMirror 6 TOML editor that is the primary interface for editing .formula.toml files. This is the central component of the formula editor route and the foundation for the auto-save hook and variables panel.

Files to create:

If no suitable TOML extension exists, fall back to plain text mode — TOML syntax highlighting is nice-to-have, not blocking. The smol-toml parser handles all structural validation regardless of editor highlighting.
- smol-toml library for TOML parsing (small, fast, ESM-native)

- Editor fills the main panel of the app shell

{"id":"bcc-6not9.4.2","title":"Graph visualization with metrics","description":"Build the interactive dependency graph visualization using the library selected in Task 2C.1 (see beads-ide/docs/graph-library-decision.md for the selected library and benchmark results). Key details:
- Node shape by bead type: hexagon = epic, circle = task (per spec)
- Edge style by dependency type: solid = blocks, dashed = related
- Three layout algorithms: force-directed (default), hierarchical, manual (positions saved in SessionState localStorage)

- 9 metrics sourced from bv --robot-insights via GET /api/graph/metrics

{"id":"bcc-6not9.2.1","title":"Formula routes","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-fjvy2
attached_at: 2026-02-22T02:35:18Z
dispatched_by: bcc/crew/ed

Implement the backend REST API routes for formula CRUD, cook, and sling operations. All CLI invocations use the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts with full input validation.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/formulas.ts — formula route handlers
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/formulas.test.ts — integration tests

Endpoints:
- GET /api/formulas — scan all configured search paths (formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/), return FormulaFile[] with name, path, directory
- GET /api/formulas/:name — read and return formula TOML content + parsed structure
- PUT /api/formulas/:name — write formula TOML to disk (target for auto-save)
- POST /api/formulas/:name/cook — invoke bd cook <path> --json, return CookResult

{"id":"bcc-6not9.5.3","title":"Accessibility pass (WCAG 2.1 AA)","description":"Conduct a comprehensive accessibility audit and remediation pass across all components to meet WCAG 2.1 AA. This is a cross-cutting pass over multiple component files in beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/.

Files to update (known remediation targets):
- components/layout/command-palette.tsx — focus trap, keyboard nav
- components/layout/formula-tree.tsx — keyboard nav, aria-expanded
- components/formulas/pour-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/formulas/sling-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/beads/bead-status-badge.tsx — shape + icon (not color-only)
- components/beads/bead-detail.tsx — heading hierarchy, aria-labels
- components/results/graph-view.tsx — list/tree alternative, aria-labels
- All icon-only buttons across components/ — add aria-label
- All loading states — add aria-live regions

Key details per spec:
- Screen reader alternative for graph: list/tree view accessible to screen readers
- Shape + icon as primary differentiators (not color alone) for node types and bead status
- Keyboard navigation for all operations (no mouse-only actions)
- Proper heading hierarchy throughout
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons
- aria-live regions for loading states
- Focus management in command palette and dialogs (focus trap)
- type="button" on all non-submit buttons
- Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- Terminology enforcement: verify "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", "dependency" not "bond"
- Terminology: show only top-4 dependency types in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- Terminology: hide BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) — "Phase" refers only to bead lifecycle
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All interactive elements keyboard-navigable
- [ ] Screen reader can navigate formula tree, bead list
- [ ] Graph has list/tree alternative view
- [ ] No color-only encoding (shapes + icons always present)
- [ ] Command palette traps focus correctly
- [ ] Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- [ ] Terminology enforcement verified: "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", etc.
- [ ] Only top-4 dependency types shown in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- [ ] BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) not exposed to users","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:15Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:15Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Create a secure backend utility for invoking bd, gt, and bv CLI commands without shell injection vulnerabilities. This wrapper is used by all backend route handlers.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts — execFile wrapper with allowlist validation
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/cli.test.ts — unit tests

Key details:
- Use Node.js child_process.execFile (NOT exec) — no shell interpolation
- Validate inputs: formula names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$, variable keys against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$, variable values against ^[^\x00-\x1f]+$ (no control chars)
- Set cwd to project root where .beads/redirect resolves
- Default timeout: 30s; cook timeout: 60s
- Return { stdout, stderr, exitCode }
- CLI flag verification (prerequisite for 2A.1/2A.3): Run bd cook formulas/explore-module.formula.toml --json and check if stdout is valid JSON. Run bv --export-graph - --output-format json and check if stdout is JSON (not HTML). If --json flag is not recognized by bd cook, fallback: parse the plain-text stdout from bd cook line-by-line. If --output-format json is not supported by bv --export-graph, fallback: bv may write an HTML file — parse the graph data from the HTML or use bv --robot-insights --output-format json only. Document findings in beads-ide/docs/cli-flags.md.","notes":""}

{"description":"Create the Hono HTTP server that serves as the backend for the Beads IDE. The server proxies CLI commands and exposes a typed REST API to the frontend. It must bind to 127.0.0.1 only (not 0.0.0.0) per spec security requirements.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/index.ts — Hono app, bind 127.0.0.1:3001, register routes
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/config.ts — load formula search paths, CLI binary locations, project root detection
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts — GET /api/health verifies bd CLI is available
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tsconfig.json — extends base TypeScript config

Key details:
- Use @hono/node-server for the Node.js runtime adapter
- Config resolves formula search paths in order: formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/; skip missing directories gracefully
- GET /api/health returns { ok: true, bd_version: "..." }
- GET /api/config returns { formula_paths: [...], project_root: "...", ... }","notes":""}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.4.3","title":"Dense graph handling","description":"Add simplification strategies to the graph view for handling 50-200 bead dense graphs. Extends graph-view.tsx and graph-controls.tsx created in task 4B.1.

Files to update:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-view.tsx — add clustering and focus mode
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-controls.tsx — add simplification toggles

Key details:
- Epic clustering: collapse an epic's child beads into a single cluster node to reduce visible node count
- Focus mode: click a node to show N-hop neighborhood (default N=2); all other nodes dimmed/hidden
- Semantic zoom: show less detail when zoomed out (hide labels, simplify edges)
- Fisheye distortion: magnify area around cursor while compressing periphery (optional simplification mode)
- Density indicator in a health panel: warn at >0.10 density, red at >0.12
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Epic clustering reduces visible nodes
- [ ] Focus mode shows neighborhood of selected node
- [ ] Fisheye distortion available as simplification option
- [ ] Density indicator shows correct value
- [ ] Density warnings at thresholds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:13Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:13Z"}

{"id":"bcc-6not9.5.2","title":"Error handling for all failure modes","description":"Implement robust error handling for all failure modes identified in the spec Error Handling table. Ensures the IDE degrades gracefully rather than showing blank screens or crashing.

Files to update:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts — connection error detection, offline/degraded mode signaling
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx — React error boundary

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/error-page.tsx — "Cannot connect to backend" full-page error
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/offline-banner.tsx — "Database unavailable" degraded mode banner

Failure mode matrix (from spec):
- Backend not running -> error page (not blank screen)
- Cook failure -> inline error with stderr in cook preview panel
- Sling failure -> toast.error() with retry
- Database unavailable -> degraded mode: formula editing still works, bead data unavailable banner shown

attached_at: 2026-02-22T03:12:47Z
dispatched_by: bcc/crew/ed

Conduct a comprehensive accessibility audit and remediation pass across all components to meet WCAG 2.1 AA. This is a cross-cutting pass over multiple component files in beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/.

Files to update (known remediation targets):
- components/layout/command-palette.tsx — focus trap, keyboard nav
- components/layout/formula-tree.tsx — keyboard nav, aria-expanded
- components/formulas/pour-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/formulas/sling-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/beads/bead-status-badge.tsx — shape + icon (not color-only)
- components/beads/bead-detail.tsx — heading hierarchy, aria-labels
- components/results/graph-view.tsx — list/tree alternative, aria-labels
- All icon-only buttons across components/ — add aria-label
- All loading states — add aria-live regions

Key details per spec:
- Screen reader alternative for graph: list/tree view accessible to screen readers
- Shape + icon as primary differentiators (not color alone) for node types and bead status
- Keyboard navigation for all operations (no mouse-only actions)
- Proper heading hierarchy throughout
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons
- aria-live regions for loading states
- Focus management in command palette and dialogs (focus trap)
- type="button" on all non-submit buttons
- Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- Terminology enforcement: verify "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", "dependency" not "bond"
- Terminology: show only top-4 dependency types in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- Terminology: hide BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) — "Phase" refers only to bead lifecycle","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All interactive elements keyboard-navigable
- [ ] Screen reader can navigate formula tree, bead list
- [ ] Graph has list/tree alternative view
- [ ] No color-only encoding (shapes + icons always present)
- [ ] Command palette traps focus correctly
- [ ] Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- [ ] Terminology enforcement verified: "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", etc.
- [ ] Only top-4 dependency types shown in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- [ ] BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) not exposed to users","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"bcc/polecats/nitro","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:15Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:12:48Z"}

{"description":"Create the npm workspaces monorepo with frontend, backend, and shared packages for the Beads IDE project. This establishes the project structure that all subsequent tasks build upon.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/package.json — npm workspaces root (apps/frontend, apps/backend, packages/shared)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/package.json — React 19 + Vite + TanStack Router + Tailwind v4
- beads-ide/apps/backend/package.json — Hono + @hono/node-server + tsx (dev runner)
- beads-ide/packages/shared/package.json — TypeScript types shared between FE/BE
- beads-ide/tsconfig.base.json — strict TypeScript base config with path aliases
- beads-ide/biome.json — Biome config for linting and formatting
- beads-ide/.gitignore — node_modules, dist, .env, etc.

Key details:
- All packages use TypeScript 5.x with strict mode
- Scripts: "dev" starts both apps concurrently, "build" builds all, "check" runs Biome
- Named exports only (no default exports) — enforced by Biome rule
- Shared package exports types via TypeScript paths, NOT via npm pack/build
"}

- beads-ide/package.json — npm workspaces root (apps/frontend, apps/backend, packages/shared)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/package.json — React 19 + Vite + TanStack Router + Tailwind v4
- beads-ide/apps/backend/package.json — Hono + @hono/node-server + tsx (dev runner)
- beads-ide/packages/shared/package.json — TypeScript types shared between FE/BE
- beads-ide/tsconfig.base.json — strict TypeScript base config with path aliases
- beads-ide/biome.json — Biome config for linting and formatting
- beads-ide/.gitignore — node_modules, dist, .env, etc.

Key details:
- All packages use TypeScript 5.x with strict mode
- Scripts: "dev" starts both apps concurrently, "build" builds all, "check" runs Biome
- Named exports only (no default exports) — enforced by Biome rule
- Shared package exports types via TypeScript paths, NOT via npm pack/build

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.1.2","title":"Define shared TypeScript types","description":"Create all shared TypeScript type definitions for the Beads IDE. Types are derived from docs/beads.md (Go Issue struct, lines 17-75) and docs/formulas.md (Go Formula struct, lines 1-250) and must match the actual JSON output shape of bd list --json and bd show --json.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/types.ts — core domain types: Bead, BeadStatus, BeadType, Dependency, DependencyType, Formula, VarDef, Step, Gate, LoopSpec, OnCompleteSpec, ComposeRules
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts — IDE-specific types: Wave, GraphNode, GraphMetrics, GraphStats, FormulaFile, CookResult, ApiResponse, CliInvocation, SessionState, BeadsIDEConfig
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/index.ts — barrel export for all types

Key details: Types are the single source of truth consumed by both frontend (all components) and backend (route handlers, CLI response parsing). Derive all type definitions directly from docs/beads.md lines 17-75 (Go Issue struct -> Bead type, status/type enums, dependency struct) and docs/formulas.md lines 1-250 (Go Formula struct -> Formula type, VarDef, Step, Gate, etc.). Run bd list --json | head -1 and bd show <any-id> --json to verify type shapes match actual CLI output.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All types compile with strict TypeScript
- [ ] Types match actual bd list --json output (verify with a manual JSON sample)
- [ ] Types exported and importable from both frontend and backend","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:29Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:16:32Z"}

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/vite.config.ts — dev server on port 5173, proxy /api -> localhost:3001, @/ path alias

- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/app.css — Tailwind v4 imports, brand-* CSS variables, dark mode
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx — root layout (placeholder shell)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/index.tsx — landing page (placeholder)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/utils.ts — cn() utility (clsx + twMerge)
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts — typed fetch wrapper returning ApiResponse<T>
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/tsconfig.json — extends base config, includes path aliases

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.1.6","title":"Set up backend API server","description":"Create the Hono HTTP server that serves as the backend for the Beads IDE. Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/index.ts — Hono app, bind 127.0.0.1:3001, register routes
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/config.ts — load formula search paths, CLI binary locations, project root detection

Create the Hono HTTP server that serves as the backend for the Beads IDE. The server proxies CLI commands and exposes a typed REST API to the frontend. It must bind to 127.0.0.1 only (not 0.0.0.0) per spec security requirements.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/index.ts — Hono app, bind 127.0.0.1:3001, register routes
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/config.ts — load formula search paths, CLI binary locations, project root detection
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts — GET /api/health verifies bd CLI is available
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tsconfig.json — extends base TypeScript config

Key details:
- Use @hono/node-server for the Node.js runtime adapter
- Config resolves formula search paths in order: formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/; skip missing directories gracefully
- GET /api/health returns { ok: true, bd_version: "..." }
- GET /api/config returns { formula_paths: [...], project_root: "...", ... }"}

{"description":"Create the Hono HTTP server that serves as the backend for the Beads IDE. The server proxies CLI commands and exposes a typed REST API to the frontend. It must bind to 127.0.0.1 only (not 0.0.0.0) per spec security requirements.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/index.ts — Hono app, bind 127.0.0.1:3001, register routes
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/config.ts — load formula search paths, CLI binary locations, project root detection
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/health.ts — GET /api/health verifies bd CLI is available
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tsconfig.json — extends base TypeScript config

Key details:
- Use @hono/node-server for the Node.js runtime adapter
- Config resolves formula search paths in order: formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/; skip missing directories gracefully
- GET /api/health returns { ok: true, bd_version: "..." }
- GET /api/config returns { formula_paths: [...], project_root: "...", ... }"}

{"id":"bcc-28k7j","title":"Build broken on integration/beads-ide: @tailwindcss/vite missing","description":"## Pre-existing build failure on integration/beads-ide

Introduced by commit 8f9c8a4 (feat(beads-ide): Set up Vite frontend skeleton with Tailwind v4) from chrome's bcc-jumu6.1.5 branch.

**Error:**
```
failed to load config from beads-ide/apps/frontend/vite.config.ts
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package '@tailwindcss/vite'
imported from .../apps/frontend/node_modules/.vite-temp/vite.config.ts.timestamp-*.mjs
```

**Root cause:** vite.config.ts imports `@tailwindcss/vite` but the package is not installed in beads-ide/apps/frontend/package.json or node_modules.

**Fix:** Add `@tailwindcss/vite` to beads-ide/apps/frontend/package.json devDependencies and run `npm install`.

**Impact:**
- integration/beads-ide build is currently failing
- Blocked: polecat/nitro/bcc-jumu6.1.6 may have been incorrectly rejected due to this pre-existing failure
- Detected at: 2026-02-22T06:23:23Z","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:23:23Z","created_by":"bcc/refinery","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:23:23Z"}

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.2.1","title":"Formula routes","description":"Implement the backend REST API routes for formula CRUD, cook, and sling operations. All CLI invocations use the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts with full input validation.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/formulas.ts — formula route handlers
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/formulas.test.ts — integration tests

Endpoints:
- GET /api/formulas — scan all configured search paths (formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/), return FormulaFile[] with name, path, directory
- GET /api/formulas/:name — read and return formula TOML content + parsed structure
- PUT /api/formulas/:name — write formula TOML to disk (target for auto-save)
- POST /api/formulas/:name/cook — invoke bd cook <path> --json, return CookResult
- POST /api/formulas/:name/sling — invoke gt sling <formula> <target>, return status

Key details: Formula names validated against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$ before any CLI invocation. Invalid names return 400 with error message.
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] List returns formulas from all 4 search paths
- [ ] Cook returns proto beads JSON or error with stderr
- [ ] Sling invokes gt sling with validated arguments

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.2.3","title":"Graph routes","description":"Implement backend API routes that proxy bv CLI commands to expose graph metrics and graph export data. The backend normalizes bv output into typed GraphMetrics shapes defined in beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/graph.ts — graph route handlers
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/graph.test.ts — integration tests

- GET /api/graph/metrics — invoke bv --robot-insights --output-format json
- GET /api/graph/export — invoke bv --export-graph - --output-format json (stdout export)

Key details: Parse bv output into GraphMetrics types. The 9 graph metrics are: PageRank, betweenness centrality, HITS (authority + hub), critical path length, eigenvector centrality, degree (in/out), density, cycle count, and topological sort order. Map each to a named property on the GraphMetrics interface from beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts. If bv binary is missing, return a structured error response (not a crash) — missing bv is a gracefully handled degraded state. Uses the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts.

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.1.2","title":"Define shared TypeScript types","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-w71949
attached_at: 2026-02-22T06:14:29Z
dispatched_by: deacon

Create all shared TypeScript type definitions for the Beads IDE. Types are derived from docs/beads.md (Go Issue struct, lines 17-75) and docs/formulas.md (Go Formula struct, lines 1-250) and must match the actual JSON output shape of bd list --json and bd show --json.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/types.ts — core domain types: Bead, BeadStatus, BeadType, Dependency, DependencyType, Formula, VarDef, Step, Gate, LoopSpec, OnCompleteSpec, ComposeRules
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/ide-types.ts — IDE-specific types: Wave, GraphNode, GraphMetrics, GraphStats, FormulaFile, CookResult, ApiResponse, CliInvocation, SessionState, BeadsIDEConfig
- beads-ide/packages/shared/src/index.ts — barrel export for all types

Key details: Types are the single source of truth consumed by both frontend (all components) and backend (route handlers, CLI response parsing). Derive all type definitions directly from docs/beads.md lines 17-75 (Go Issue struct -> Bead type, status/type enums, dependency struct) and docs/formulas.md lines 1-250 (Go Formula struct -> Formula type, VarDef, Step, Gate, etc.). Run bd list --json | head -1 and bd show <any-id> --json to verify type shapes match actual CLI output.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All types compile with strict TypeScript
- [ ] Types match actual bd list --json output (verify with a manual JSON sample)
- [ ] Types exported and importable from both frontend and backend","notes":"Implemented shared TypeScript types for Beads IDE. Added backward-compatible types for backend/frontend: BeadApiError, BeadFull, BeadShowResponse, BeadsListResponse, CookRequest, RankedMetric, DegreeMetrics, GraphMetricsResult, GraphExport, GraphExportResult, GraphEdge, FormulaVariable, ProtoBead, BeadDependent. Extended Bead type with needs/depends_on/count fields. Fixed frontend to use FormulaListItem. All 20 shared package tests pass, typecheck clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"bcc/polecats/rust","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:29Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:57:57Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:57:57Z","close_reason":"Closed"}

Implement the backend REST API routes for formula CRUD, cook, and sling operations. All CLI invocations use the cli.ts wrapper from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/cli.ts with full input validation.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/formulas.ts — formula route handlers
- beads-ide/apps/backend/tests/routes/formulas.test.ts — integration tests

Endpoints:
- GET /api/formulas — scan all configured search paths (formulas/, .beads/formulas/, ~/.beads/formulas/, $GT_ROOT/.beads/formulas/), return FormulaFile[] with name, path, directory
- GET /api/formulas/:name — read and return formula TOML content + parsed structure
- PUT /api/formulas/:name — write formula TOML to disk (target for auto-save)
- POST /api/formulas/:name/cook — invoke bd cook <path> --json, return CookResult
- POST /api/formulas/:name/sling — invoke gt sling <formula> <target>, return status

Key details: Formula names validated against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$ before any CLI invocation. Invalid names return 400 with error message.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] List returns formulas from all 4 search paths
- [ ] Cook returns proto beads JSON or error with stderr

- Validate parsed TOML against Formula type shape; show inline diagnostics for parse errors
- Editor fills the main panel of the app shell

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.3.3","title":"Auto-save with debounce","description":"Implement debounced auto-save that writes the formula to disk 500ms after the user stops typing. Calls PUT /api/formulas/:name from beads-ide/apps/backend/src/routes/formulas.ts.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/hooks/use-auto-save.ts — debounced save hook

Key details:
- useEffect + setTimeout(500ms) pattern; clear timeout on each new keystroke (debounce reset)
- Calls PUT /api/formulas/:name with current TOML content via beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts
- Show save state indicator: subtle "Saving..." -> "Saved" text or checkmark icon
- Save errors surface via toast.error() (Sonner)
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Saves 500ms after last keystroke
- [ ] No save during active typing (debounce resets)
- [ ] Save failure shows toast error
- [ ] Visual indicator of save state (saving/saved/error)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:35Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:03:08Z"}

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.5.2","title":"Error handling for all failure modes","description":"Implement robust error handling for all failure modes identified in the spec Error Handling table. Ensures the IDE degrades gracefully rather than showing blank screens or crashing.

Files to update:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts — connection error detection, offline/degraded mode signaling
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx — React error boundary

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/error-page.tsx — "Cannot connect to backend" full-page error
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/ui/offline-banner.tsx — "Database unavailable" degraded mode banner

Failure mode matrix (from spec):
- Backend not running -> error page (not blank screen)
- Cook failure -> inline error with stderr in cook preview panel
- Sling failure -> toast.error() with retry
- Database unavailable -> degraded mode: formula editing still works, bead data unavailable banner shown
- Formula validation error -> inline highlighting in editor
- Wave cycle detected -> warning banner + list fallback

Key details: All fetch errors flow through beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/lib/api.ts which returns ApiResponse<T> with { data, error } envelope. Use try/catch with finally for loading state reset (openedi pattern). User-facing errors via toast.error() (Sonner).
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Backend down -> error page renders (not blank screen)
- [ ] Cook failure shows stderr inline
- [ ] Sling failure shows toast with error details
- [ ] Formula editing works even when database is down","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:40Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:03:09Z"}

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.4.2","title":"Graph visualization with metrics","description":"Build the interactive dependency graph visualization using the library selected in Task 2C.1 (see beads-ide/docs/graph-library-decision.md for the selected library and benchmark results). Overlays 9 graph metrics from GET /api/graph/metrics on node appearance.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-view.tsx — graph canvas component
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-controls.tsx — layout switcher, metric selector, zoom controls
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/hooks/use-graph.ts — graph data + metrics from API

Key details:
- Node shape by bead type: hexagon = epic, circle = task (per spec)
- Edge style by dependency type: solid = blocks, dashed = related
- Three layout algorithms: force-directed (default), hierarchical, manual (positions saved in SessionState localStorage)
- Metrics overlay: selecting a metric changes node size/color to reflect metric value
- 9 metrics sourced from bv --robot-insights via GET /api/graph/metrics
- Dense graph handling: auto-cluster by epic at >30 visible nodes, focus mode (N-hop neighborhood, default N=2)
- Clicking a node opens the bead detail panel from 3C.2

- components/formulas/sling-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/beads/bead-status-badge.tsx — shape + icon (not color-only)

- components/results/graph-view.tsx — list/tree alternative, aria-labels

- All loading states — add aria-live regions

Key details per spec:
- Screen reader alternative for graph: list/tree view accessible to screen readers
- Shape + icon as primary differentiators (not color alone) for node types and bead status
- Keyboard navigation for all operations (no mouse-only actions)
- Proper heading hierarchy throughout
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons
- aria-live regions for loading states
- Focus management in command palette and dialogs (focus trap)
- type="button" on all non-submit buttons
- Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- Terminology enforcement: verify "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", "dependency" not "bond"
- Terminology: show only top-4 dependency types in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- Terminology: hide BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) — "Phase" refers only to bead lifecycle

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.4.2","title":"Graph visualization with metrics","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-qo3qq3
attached_at: 2026-02-22T07:55:01Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Build the interactive dependency graph visualization using the library selected in Task 2C.1 (see beads-ide/docs/graph-library-decision.md for the selected library and benchmark results). Overlays 9 graph metrics from GET /api/graph/metrics on node appearance.

Files to create:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-view.tsx — graph canvas component
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-controls.tsx — layout switcher, metric selector, zoom controls
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/hooks/use-graph.ts — graph data + metrics from API

Key details:
- Node shape by bead type: hexagon = epic, circle = task (per spec)
- Edge style by dependency type: solid = blocks, dashed = related
- Three layout algorithms: force-directed (default), hierarchical, manual (positions saved in SessionState localStorage)
- Metrics overlay: selecting a metric changes node size/color to reflect metric value
- 9 metrics sourced from bv --robot-insights via GET /api/graph/metrics
- Dense graph handling: auto-cluster by epic at >30 visible nodes, focus mode (N-hop neighborhood, default N=2)
- Clicking a node opens the bead detail panel from 3C.2","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Renders 200 beads in <1s (Chrome, 1280x720)
- [ ] Pan/zoom/drag <100ms
- [ ] Three layout algorithms switch correctly
- [ ] Metrics overlay changes node appearance
- [ ] Node shapes differ by bead type
- [ ] Edge styles differ by dependency type
- [ ] Click node opens bead detail panel","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:38Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T07:55:04Z"}

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.4.3","title":"Dense graph handling","description":"Add simplification strategies to the graph view for handling 50-200 bead dense graphs. Extends graph-view.tsx and graph-controls.tsx created in task 4B.1.

Files to update:
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-view.tsx — add clustering and focus mode
- beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-controls.tsx — add simplification toggles

Key details:
- Epic clustering: collapse an epic's child beads into a single cluster node to reduce visible node count
- Focus mode: click a node to show N-hop neighborhood (default N=2); all other nodes dimmed/hidden
- Semantic zoom: show less detail when zoomed out (hide labels, simplify edges)
- Fisheye distortion: magnify area around cursor while compressing periphery (optional simplification mode)
- Density indicator in a health panel: warn at >0.10 density, red at >0.12
","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Epic clustering reduces visible nodes
- [ ] Focus mode shows neighborhood of selected node
- [ ] Fisheye distortion available as simplification option
- [ ] Density indicator shows correct value
- [ ] Density warnings at thresholds","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:38Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T03:03:08Z"}

{"id":"bcc-jumu6.5.3","title":"Accessibility pass (WCAG 2.1 AA)","description":"attached_molecule: bcc-wisp-wisp-uxdnh8
attached_at: 2026-02-22T09:18:40Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Conduct a comprehensive accessibility audit and remediation pass across all components to meet WCAG 2.1 AA. This is a cross-cutting pass over multiple component files in beads-ide/apps/frontend/src/components/.

Files to update (known remediation targets):
- components/layout/command-palette.tsx — focus trap, keyboard nav
- components/layout/formula-tree.tsx — keyboard nav, aria-expanded
- components/formulas/pour-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/formulas/sling-dialog.tsx — focus trap
- components/beads/bead-status-badge.tsx — shape + icon (not color-only)
- components/beads/bead-detail.tsx — heading hierarchy, aria-labels
- components/results/graph-view.tsx — list/tree alternative, aria-labels
- All icon-only buttons across components/ — add aria-label
- All loading states — add aria-live regions

Key details per spec:
- Screen reader alternative for graph: list/tree view accessible to screen readers
- Shape + icon as primary differentiators (not color alone) for node types and bead status
- Keyboard navigation for all operations (no mouse-only actions)
- Proper heading hierarchy throughout
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons
- aria-live regions for loading states
- Focus management in command palette and dialogs (focus trap)
- type="button" on all non-submit buttons
- Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- Terminology enforcement: verify "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", "dependency" not "bond"
- Terminology: show only top-4 dependency types in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- Terminology: hide BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) — "Phase" refers only to bead lifecycle","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All interactive elements keyboard-navigable
- [ ] Screen reader can navigate formula tree, bead list
- [ ] Graph has list/tree alternative view
- [ ] No color-only encoding (shapes + icons always present)
- [ ] Command palette traps focus correctly
- [ ] Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- [ ] Terminology enforcement verified: "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", etc.
- [ ] Only top-4 dependency types shown in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- [ ] BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) not exposed to users","status":"hooked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"bcc/polecats/rust","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-22T01:27:40Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-22T09:18:40Z"}

- components/beads/bead-detail.tsx — heading hierarchy, aria-labels
- components/results/graph-view.tsx — list/tree alternative, aria-labels
- All icon-only buttons across components/ — add aria-label
- All loading states — add aria-live regions

Key details per spec:
- Screen reader alternative for graph: list/tree view accessible to screen readers
- Shape + icon as primary differentiators (not color alone) for node types and bead status
- Keyboard navigation for all operations (no mouse-only actions)
- Proper heading hierarchy throughout
- aria-label on all icon-only buttons
- aria-live regions for loading states
- Focus management in command palette and dialogs (focus trap)
- type="button" on all non-submit buttons
- Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- Terminology enforcement: verify "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", "dependency" not "bond"
- Terminology: show only top-4 dependency types in UI (blocks, related, parent-child, duplicate)
- Terminology: hide BCC compilation phases (SCAN/ANALYZE/CONNECT/ENRICH) — "Phase" refers only to bead lifecycle","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] All interactive elements keyboard-navigable

- [ ] Command palette traps focus correctly
- [ ] Skeleton loading used for all data-dependent panels (no full-screen spinners)
- [ ] Terminology enforcement verified: "Cook" not "Compile", "Wave" not "Level", etc.

{"id":"bcc-mol-s6kab","title":"Consolidate review findings","description":"Merge the three review results into a single consolidated report.

## Input Files

Read all available review files:
- `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-forward.tmp` — plan→beads coverage (REQUIRED)
- `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-reverse.tmp` — beads→plan traceability (REQUIRED)
- `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-deps.tmp` — dependency integrity (REQUIRED)

## Consolidation Process

### 1. Cross-Reference Findings

Look for the same issue found from multiple angles:
- A missing bead (forward) with no plan basis for surrounding beads (reverse)
  → Systematic conversion gap, escalate severity
- Over-constrained deps (deps review) on a task that's also gold-plated (reverse)
  → Stronger case for removing the bead entirely
- Missing blocker (deps) for a partially-matched bead (forward)
  → The bead needs both content and dependency fixes

Issues found by multiple reviewers get a **[CORROBORATED]** tag and elevated priority.

### 2. Deduplicate

Where multiple reviewers found the same underlying issue:
- Keep the most detailed description
- Note which reviewers independently identified it
- Use the highest severity from any reviewer

### 3. Categorize All Findings

Group into these categories:

**Coverage Gaps** (from forward review)
- Plan tasks with no matching bead
- Beads with significant content loss from the plan task

**Conversion Scope Creep** (from reverse review)
- Beads with no plan basis
- Gold-plating (bead adds requirements beyond plan)

**Dependency Errors** (from deps review)
- Missing blockers (tasks can start before prerequisites)
- Over-constrained dependencies (false serialization)
- Structural issues (orphans, hierarchy problems)

**Content Fidelity** (cross-review)
- Description drift between plan and bead
- Acceptance criteria mismatch
- Priority/label inconsistencies

### 4. Severity Assignment

- **P0 — Must Fix:** Missing beads for plan tasks, missing blockers, unbacked beads with significant scope
- **P1 — Should Fix:** Significant content loss, over-constrained deps, gold-plating
- **P2 — Consider:** Minor content gaps, granularity issues, label/priority tweaks

### 5. Actionability

For each finding, determine the specific bd command(s) to fix it:

- **Create missing bead:** `bd create "Title" -t task -d "..." --parent <epic-id>`
- **Add missing dependency:** `bd dep add <blocker-id> <blocked-id>`
- **Remove false dependency:** `bd dep remove <blocker-id> <blocked-id>`
- **Update bead content:** `bd update <id> -d "..." --acceptance "..."`
- **Close unbacked bead:** `bd close <id>` with rationale
- **Reparent bead:** Move to correct epic

## Output

Write consolidated report to: `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/beads-review.md`

Use this format:
```markdown
# Beads Review: explorer-onboarding

**Generated:** [date]
**Reviewers:** Forward (plan→beads), Reverse (beads→plan), Dependencies (graph integrity)

---

## Summary

| Category | P0 | P1 | P2 | Total |
|----------|----|----|----|----|
| Coverage Gaps | N | N | N | N |
| Conversion Scope Creep | N | N | N | N |
| Dependency Errors | N | N | N | N |
| Content Fidelity | N | N | N | N |
| **Total** | **N** | **N** | **N** | **N** |

---

## P0 Findings (Must Fix)

### 1. [Finding Title] [CORROBORATED]
- **Category:** Coverage Gap / Scope Creep / Dependency Error
- **Found by:** Forward + Dependencies (corroborated)
- **What:** [Description]
- **Evidence:** [What the reviewers found]
- **Fix command(s):**
  ```bash
  bd create "Missing task title" -t task -d "Description" --parent <epic-id>
  bd dep add <blocker-id> <new-id>
  ```

---

## P1 Findings (Should Fix)

### N. [Finding Title]
- **Category:** [Category]
- **Found by:** [Which reviewer(s)]
- **What:** [Description]
- **Fix command(s):** [Specific bd commands]

---

## P2 Findings (Consider)

### N. [Finding Title]
- **Category:** [Category]
- **Found by:** [Which reviewer(s)]
- **What:** [Description]
- **Fix suggestion:** [What to do]

---

## Parallelism Report

- **Dependency waves:** N
- **Maximum parallel width:** N beads
- **Critical path:** N beads
- **Ready queue:** [list of beads ready to start]

## Coverage Summary

**Forward (Plan→Beads):**
- Fully matched: N tasks
- Partially matched: N tasks
- No matching bead: N tasks

**Reverse (Beads→Plan):**
- Plan-backed: N beads
- Plan-implied: N beads
- Scope creep: N beads

**Dependencies:**
- Correctly constrained: N
- Missing blockers: N
- Over-constrained: N
```

After writing the report, delete ALL .tmp files (including the beads snapshot):
```bash
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-forward.tmp
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-reverse.tmp
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-deps.tmp
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/beads-snapshot.tmp
```

Files:
- Create: /apps/backend/src/routes/workspace.ts

Endpoints:
- GET /api/workspace: returns WorkspaceStateResponse or WorkspaceError with NO_ROOT
- POST /api/workspace/open: validates path, auto-creates .beads/ if missing, calls setWorkspaceRoot
- POST /api/workspace/init: scaffolds .beads/ + formulas/ dirs, writes blank template
- GET /api/tree: recursive async scan, prunes empty dirs, 500 node limit
- GET /api/browse: directory listing for browser modal

Patterns: Follow formulas.ts pattern, use .js imports, async FS, path.resolve validation.","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] curl localhost:3001/api/workspace returns current state JSON

- [ ] curl localhost:3001/api/browse?path=/home returns directory listing
- [ ] POST to non-existent path returns { ok: false, code: 'NOT_FOUND' } with HTTP 400","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"krgebis@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:00:56Z","created_by":"bcc/crew/krystian","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:00:56Z"}

- Modify: /apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx

{"id":"bcc-mol-s6kab","title":"Consolidate review findings","description":"Merge the three review results into a single consolidated report.

## Input Files

Read all available review files:
- `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-forward.tmp` — plan→beads coverage (REQUIRED)
- `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-reverse.tmp` — beads→plan traceability (REQUIRED)
- `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-deps.tmp` — dependency integrity (REQUIRED)

## Consolidation Process

### 1. Cross-Reference Findings

Look for the same issue found from multiple angles:
- A missing bead (forward) with no plan basis for surrounding beads (reverse)
  → Systematic conversion gap, escalate severity
- Over-constrained deps (deps review) on a task that's also gold-plated (reverse)
  → Stronger case for removing the bead entirely
- Missing blocker (deps) for a partially-matched bead (forward)
  → The bead needs both content and dependency fixes

Issues found by multiple reviewers get a **[CORROBORATED]** tag and elevated priority.

### 2. Deduplicate

Where multiple reviewers found the same underlying issue:
- Keep the most detailed description
- Note which reviewers independently identified it
- Use the highest severity from any reviewer

### 3. Categorize All Findings

Group into these categories:

**Coverage Gaps** (from forward review)
- Plan tasks with no matching bead
- Beads with significant content loss from the plan task

**Conversion Scope Creep** (from reverse review)
- Beads with no plan basis
- Gold-plating (bead adds requirements beyond plan)

**Dependency Errors** (from deps review)
- Missing blockers (tasks can start before prerequisites)
- Over-constrained dependencies (false serialization)
- Structural issues (orphans, hierarchy problems)

**Content Fidelity** (cross-review)
- Description drift between plan and bead
- Acceptance criteria mismatch
- Priority/label inconsistencies

### 4. Severity Assignment

- **P0 — Must Fix:** Missing beads for plan tasks, missing blockers, unbacked beads with significant scope
- **P1 — Should Fix:** Significant content loss, over-constrained deps, gold-plating
- **P2 — Consider:** Minor content gaps, granularity issues, label/priority tweaks

### 5. Actionability

For each finding, determine the specific bd command(s) to fix it:

- **Create missing bead:** `bd create "Title" -t task -d "..." --parent <epic-id>`
- **Add missing dependency:** `bd dep add <blocker-id> <blocked-id>`
- **Remove false dependency:** `bd dep remove <blocker-id> <blocked-id>`
- **Update bead content:** `bd update <id> -d "..." --acceptance "..."`
- **Close unbacked bead:** `bd close <id>` with rationale
- **Reparent bead:** Move to correct epic

## Output

Write consolidated report to: `plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/beads-review.md`

Use this format:
```markdown
# Beads Review: explorer-onboarding

**Generated:** [date]
**Reviewers:** Forward (plan→beads), Reverse (beads→plan), Dependencies (graph integrity)

---

## Summary

| Category | P0 | P1 | P2 | Total |
|----------|----|----|----|----|
| Coverage Gaps | N | N | N | N |
| Conversion Scope Creep | N | N | N | N |
| Dependency Errors | N | N | N | N |
| Content Fidelity | N | N | N | N |
| **Total** | **N** | **N** | **N** | **N** |

---

## P0 Findings (Must Fix)

### 1. [Finding Title] [CORROBORATED]
- **Category:** Coverage Gap / Scope Creep / Dependency Error
- **Found by:** Forward + Dependencies (corroborated)
- **What:** [Description]
- **Evidence:** [What the reviewers found]
- **Fix command(s):**
  ```bash
  bd create "Missing task title" -t task -d "Description" --parent <epic-id>
  bd dep add <blocker-id> <new-id>
  ```

---

## P1 Findings (Should Fix)

### N. [Finding Title]
- **Category:** [Category]
- **Found by:** [Which reviewer(s)]
- **What:** [Description]
- **Fix command(s):** [Specific bd commands]

---

## P2 Findings (Consider)

### N. [Finding Title]
- **Category:** [Category]
- **Found by:** [Which reviewer(s)]
- **What:** [Description]
- **Fix suggestion:** [What to do]

---

## Parallelism Report

- **Dependency waves:** N
- **Maximum parallel width:** N beads
- **Critical path:** N beads
- **Ready queue:** [list of beads ready to start]

## Coverage Summary

**Forward (Plan→Beads):**
- Fully matched: N tasks
- Partially matched: N tasks
- No matching bead: N tasks

**Reverse (Beads→Plan):**
- Plan-backed: N beads
- Plan-implied: N beads
- Scope creep: N beads

**Dependencies:**
- Correctly constrained: N
- Missing blockers: N
- Over-constrained: N
```

After writing the report, delete ALL .tmp files (including the beads snapshot):
```bash
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-forward.tmp
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-reverse.tmp
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/review-deps.tmp
rm -f plans/explorer-onboarding/04-beads/beads-snapshot.tmp
```

Files:

{"id":"bcc-8rlwh.2.5","title":"Add startup workspace sync","description":"Effect in __root.tsx to reconcile localStorage with backend state.

Files:
- Modify: /apps/frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx

On mount: call GET /api/workspace via apiFetch, compare with localStorage.workspaceConfig.rootPath
If backend returns NO_ROOT but localStorage has root: call POST /api/workspace/open to restore
If backend root differs from localStorage: call clearConfig() and navigate to / via pushState + PopStateEvent
Use useEffect with empty dependency array","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] App syncs localStorage with backend on startup
- [ ] Backend restart (root loss) triggers welcome screen

{"id":"bcc-zgc.2","title":"Respect prefers-reduced-motion","description":"Add CSS media query handling for prefers-reduced-motion.
Affects: border transitions, chevron rotation animations, graph transitions.

Files to update:
- apps/frontend/src/components/formulas/visual-builder.tsx (node transitions)
- apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-view.tsx (graph animations)
- Global CSS or Tailwind config

Implementation: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; } }

Files to update:

- apps/frontend/src/components/results/graph-view.tsx (graph animations)
